name: Runge-Kutta 4th Order (RK4)
applicable_scenarios: non-stiff initial-value ODE systems requiring 4th-order accuracy
smooth right-hand sides where explicit methods are efficient
python_template:
import numpy as np

def rk4_ivp(f, t0, y0, t_end, h):
    ts = [t0]
    ys = [np.asarray(y0, dtype=float)]
    t = t0
    y = ys[0].copy()
    while t < t_end - 1e-12:
        k1 = np.asarray(f(t, y), dtype=float)
        k2 = np.asarray(f(t + h/2, y + h/2 * k1), dtype=float)
        k3 = np.asarray(f(t + h/2, y + h/2 * k2), dtype=float)
        k4 = np.asarray(f(t + h, y + h * k3), dtype=float)
        y = y + (h/6) * (k1 + 2*k2 + 2*k3 + k4)
        t += h
        ts.append(t)
        ys.append(y.copy())
    return np.array(ts), np.array(ys)
common_pitfalls: not appropriate for stiff systems; use implicit / BDF methods
fixed step size without error control; consider RK45 (Dormand-Prince)
cost is 4 RHS evaluations per step — prohibitive if RHS is expensive
